name: SignNow eSignature API
what_it_does: Electronic signature platform with REST API — enabling businesses to send documents for e-signature, manage document templates, embed signing into web applications, and automate document workflows, with ESIGN/UETA compliance, OAuth2 authentication, and mobile SDKs for iOS/Android signature capture.
use_cases: Embedding e-signature into web applications via SignNow API for in-app signing flows
Automating contract sending via SignNow API for sales and legal workflow automation
Managing signing templates via SignNow API for standardized document distribution
Creating embedded signing sessions via SignNow API for white-labeled signing experiences
Tracking document signing status via SignNow API for contract lifecycle management
not_for: Complex CLM with legal entity management (use DocuSign CLM for enterprise CLM)
Global eIDAS-specific compliance without additional configuration
Free e-signature (SignNow requires paid plan for API access)
best_when: A developer needs to embed e-signature into a SaaS application or automate document workflows without DocuSign pricing — SignNow offers a more affordable alternative with solid API.
avoid_when: You need enterprise CLM, complex approval workflows, or the highest-tier compliance certifications.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for server-to-server. Authorization code flow for user-delegated. Client ID + Client Secret for token request. Access token via /oauth2/token. Bearer token in Authorization header. Refresh token support. Embedded signing: create invite link with access_token → embed in iframe. Webhook events for document status. Application ID required for all API calls.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — free trial only
pricing.paid_tiers: Business $20/user/month; Business Premium $30/user/month; Enterprise custom; API plans: starts ~$150/month
pricing.notes: US company (New York). Owned by Barracuda Networks (network security company). Founded 2010. 750,000+ users. ESIGN/UETA compliant. HIPAA compliant on Enterprise. SOC2 Type II. 6 official SDKs. Embedded signing: iFrame embedding for white-label. Template creation: field placement via drag-and-drop. Bulk send. Team accounts. Mobile apps (iOS/Android). Zapier integration. Compared to DocuSign: lower pricing, fewer enterprise features. API access requires paid subscription plan. Barracuda Networks acquisition added enterprise credibility.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented for standard plans; enterprise SLAs available

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OAuth2 client credentials. 6 official SDKs. Embedded signing iframe. HIPAA on Enterprise. SOC2. Barracuda-owned. No free tier. ESIGN/UETA. Webhook events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document reads idempotent by document ID. Invite/signing status reads idempotent. Document sending not idempotent — check status before resending. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 client credentials for server apps: POST /oauth2/token with client_id, client_secret, grant_type
Embedded signing: create document → create signing invite → get sign_url for iframe embedding
Document field placement via API is complex — use template with pre-placed fields for programmatic sending
Webhook event_type names: document.complete, document.decline, document.view, etc.
Barracuda Networks ownership: primarily network security company — verify strategic commitment to e-sign
Application ID required in all API calls — not just the OAuth2 token

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 client credentials. ESIGN/UETA legal compliance. HIPAA on Enterprise (BAA required). SOC2 Type II certified. Signed documents are legally binding records (high sensitivity). US/EU data residency. Barracuda Networks ownership adds security expertise. Embedded signing in iFrame — same-origin security considerations.
